issue_title: Collision with base testsuite dependency
issue_body:
There is a version collision with `groovy-all` artifact in base testsuite when running with `auth-server-quarkus-embedded`.

Version `4.0.16` is there via `keycloak-juni5` and another version `2.4.10` is brought by `creaper-commands`.

This leads to `Conflicting module versions. Module [groovy-xml is loaded in version x.x.x and you are trying to load version y.y.y]` locally

Closes #28555

issue_title: UserStorageSyncManager int overflow
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Keycloak crashes when sync periods are set to 2147484 or greater due to an integer overflow

### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I should be able to set sync periods of greater than 2147484 without Keycloak crashing.

### Actual behavior

When sync periods are set to 2147484 or greater, an integer overflow occurs due to the multiplication, resulting in a negative delay being passed to the timer despite the checks lines [207](https://github.com/tqe1999/keycloak/blob/1bb5e1413416cbe7a83952c6a01e7f75ac7502f3/model/storage-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/managers/UserStorageSyncManager.java#L207) and [217](https://github.com/tqe1999/keycloak/blob/1bb5e1413416cbe7a83952c6a01e7f75ac7502f3/model/storage-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/managers/UserStorageSyncManager.java#L217). As a result Keycloak crashes due to the unhandled exception.

### How to Reproduce?

Set a sync period to 2147484 or greater, then restart Keycloak.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
fix integer overflow with explicit cast

Closes #28564

Signed-off-by: tqe1999 <tqe1999@gmail.com>

issue_title: Brute force detection fails with read-only LDAP users
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

The brute force detection does not permanently lock users from a read-only LDAP (it works as expected on default keycloak users)

### Version

nightly, 24.0.2, 22.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Brute force detection can also permanently disable read-only users

### Actual behavior

User is not permanently locked, instead Exception `ReadOnlyException: Federated storage is not writable` is thrown

```
2024-04-10 06:10:28,035 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services.managers.DefaultBruteForceProtector] (Brute Force Protector) failure
2024-04-10 06:10:28,035 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services.managers.DefaultBruteForceProtector] (Brute Force Protector) new num failures: 3
2024-04-10 06:10:28,036 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services.managers.DefaultBruteForceProtector] (Brute Force Protector) waitSeconds: 60
2024-04-10 06:10:28,036 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services.managers.DefaultBruteForceProtector] (Brute Force Protector) deltaTime: 3,833
2024-04-10 06:10:28,037 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services.managers.DefaultBruteForceProtector] (Brute Force Protector) user test locked permanently due to too many login attempts
2024-04-10 06:10:28,149 ERROR [org.keycloak.services] (Brute Force Protector) KC-SERVICES0052: Failed processing type: org.keycloak.storage.ReadOnlyException: Federated storage is not writable
	at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.ReadonlyLDAPUserModelDelegate.setSingleAttribute(ReadonlyLDAPUserModelDelegate.java:73)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setSingleAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:70)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setSingleAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:70)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setSingleAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:70)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setSingleAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:70)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.UserModelDelegate.setSingleAttribute(UserModelDelegate.java:70)
	at org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.UserAdapter.setSingleAttribute(UserAdapter.java:180)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.DefaultBruteForceProtector.failure(DefaultBruteForceProtector.java:218)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.DefaultBruteForceProtector.run(DefaultBruteForceProtector.java:291)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable brute force detection with permanent lockout (Realm settings -> Security defenses -> Brute force detection -> Set mode to "Lockout permanently", you may also reduce the "Max login failures" count for easier reproduction)
2. Connect a LDAP in read-only mode
3. Synchronize users from that LDAP into keycloak
4.  Trigger the brute force detection by failing the login with a LDAP user repeatedly

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix brute force detection for LDAP read-only users

Closes #28579

Signed-off-by: devjos <github_11837948@feido.de>

issue_title: Incorrect user info in the head when using lightweight access token for account-console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

Steps to reproduce:

- Login to admin console
- Go to client `account-console` and tab `Advanced` and switch `Always use lightweight access token` . Click `Save`
- Click `Manage Account` at the right top corner
- There is `Anonymous` in the header (See screenshot below).

## Cause

For retrieve user informations, the account console should use IDToken instead of access token. ID token is dedicated by OIDC specification to contain details about the user whenever access-token is used more for invoking REST API and it may not contain user info at all.

The admin console does not have this issue (it seems admin console does not use `KeycloakMasthead` utility, which has this issue).

### Version

nightly (from 2024-04-10)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Top corner should contain correct user info even if using lightweight access token for account console

### Actual behavior

Top corner contains `Anonymous` if using lightweight access token for account console

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Incorrect user info in the head when using lightweight access token for account-console
closes #28624

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: Invalide objects comparison in Java
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug
At `org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.UserStorageProviderResource` the existing code of the method `syncUsers` employs a null check followed by a direct comparison to an empty string (action == "") to ascertain if the action value is present. This method of comparison, based on reference equality, is not correct in Java for assessing string content.

### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The comparison should be based on the `equals()`method.

### Actual behavior

In Java, comparing strings with == checks if both operands refer to the same object, not if they have the same value.

### How to Reproduce?

I created a test locally

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix string comparison for action

Closes #28628

issue_title: Client roles won't open (Forbidden) with Fine Grained Permission (without view-clients realm-management role)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

When Fine Grained permission is used for Client, the "Role" inside the client can NOT be opened so the Users inside the Role can be viewed.

### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The "Roles" inside a Client to be accessible when Fine Grained permission is used with only "query-clients" realm-management role.

Animated gif (_with "view-clients" realm-management role. Because of the role, all Clients are visible, but the idea is to show that the Roles are accessible_):
![fine-grained-permission-roles-with-view-clients](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/4d7cb208-1ddc-49d1-bf36-93c901db1ed8)


### Actual behavior

"Forbidden, permission needed: view-clients" warning is returned when a Client Role is attempted to be opened:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/1c0f04d7-a05d-49cd-b539-83472fb88bec)

Animated gif:
![fine-grained-permission-roles-forbidden](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/5169b1dc-bf10-48ad-8de9-66f7ec84e0fe)


### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a user
- Assign **query-clients** **realm-management** roles
- Set a credentials, so you can sign in with it later
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/b207d2fc-bad4-4c1e-bf76-e5eb9f205d84)

2. Create a client
- Create Client Role

3. Create some test users
- Assign the users to the Client Role(s)

4. Set the Permission (at least view and manage) of the Client using a User Policy, assigning the first user created
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/825aad0f-1629-47fb-a228-009268dae17b)
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/44531e1d-9b80-4028-ae67-f885cf445824)
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/abf5745f-fbf3-4606-b6fa-a34f94c6fdd4)

5. Sign in the security-admin-console
http://localhost:8080/auth/admin/YOURREALMNAME/console/


### Anything else?

The above reported was working fine with the Old UI (Keycloak 20.0.5 for example), if there is "query-users" and "view-users" or "query-users" and Fine Grained Permissions for the users enabled:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/737290e9-98b8-43d1-9426-24acade9291b)

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/33352511/80a945d7-1b47-4407-ad19-a8ae3e3f5270)

issue_title: Accessing a transient (lightweight) user through client session fails in admin-api/-ui
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

I've enabled the `transient-users` feature and authenticated a user through an external identity provider. This works fine as expected.
But when I try to access the user in admin-ui through the (client-)sessions tab, it fails. There are several requests being called when trying to access the user and the one to

`GET /admin/realms/{realm}/ui-ext/users/lightweight-{some-uuid}/unmanagedAttributes`

fails with `404`.

### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The user details can be accessed through the (client-)sessions tab in admin-ui and no admin-api request fails.

### Actual behavior

see description, the request

`GET /admin/realms/{realm}/ui-ext/users/lightweight-{some-uuid}/unmanagedAttributes`

fails with `404`.

### How to Reproduce?

- enable the `transient-users` feature
- configure an external IdP
- authenticate a user through this IdP
- open the sessions tab in admin-ui
- locate the respective session
- click on the username of the entry to show the user details
- -> error

### Anything else?

I checked "regression", as this worked until 23, but there was no `.../unmanagedAttributes` call then. So, from the recognized behavior it's a regression, technically it's a new request which fails.

Fixes: #28666

issue_title: ID is shown prematurely on Identity Provider Mapper after Save
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When creating a new identity provider mapper, when you save the form reloads with a required ID field. The field is empty and not editable, but is shown as a required field, and you cannot Save again. If you go out and back in, the ID field is populated correctly. 

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The ID field should not appear in the form as a required field after Save... Save should probably exit out of the form and the ID field should only appear on Edit.

The ID field appears after a Save, and if you can't edit it as a required field so subsequent Saves will throw an error.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a new mapper for an identity provider, and Save. The ID will appear as a required field. Try to click Save again. Get out of the form and click the Mapper. The ID will be populated correctly.

issue_title: Webauthn Policy timeout accepts values > 8 hours
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The tooltip for the Timeout field in Authentication states that the value needs to be between 0 and 8 hours. You are able to type a values in seconds that is over the 28800 second limit (tried 28801-31299) and the error notification does not appear. 

If you enter enough values, the value will actually appear in the Seconds/Minutes/Hours dropdown, which is odd.

issue_title: Failure reset time is applied to Permanent Lockout
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

The setting (or default value) for Failure Reset Time is applied to permanent lockout. In previous versions of Keycloak this setting was only applied to temporary lockout. This setting isn't configurable for Permanent Lockout.

### Version

24

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The failure count for a given user is not reset after a time period when Brute Force with Permanent Lockout is configured.

### Actual behavior

The failure count for a given user is reset after 12 hours (or whatever was previously configured for temporary lockout) when Brute Force with Permanent Lockout is configured.

### How to Reproduce?

Enable Brute Force Protection for the realm.
Select temporary lockout.
Set Max Login Failures to 1.
Set Failure Reset Time to 5 seconds.
Save.
Select Permanent Lockout and save.
Try to login with invalid credentials.
Wait at least 5 seconds.
Try to login  with invalid credentials.
Check the stats of the user; it will not be Disabled.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Failure reset time is applied to Permanent Lockout

Closes #28821

Signed-off-by: Douglas Palmer <dpalmer@redhat.com>

issue_body:

```
HttpClientBuilder httpClientBuilder = new HttpClientBuilder(); 
httpClientBuilder
    .socketTimeout(myConnectionSocketTimeout, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
    .establishConnectionTimeout(myConnectionEstablishConnectionTimeout, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
```
In the Keycloak HttpClientBuilder these time unit values are never used, because they are not supported by the underlying Apache implementation.
This is confusing to the end user.

This bug is present since Keycloak 1.4.0.Final (9 years ago)

### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The time units are used to calculate the amount of milliseconds.
```
HttpClientBuilder httpClientBuilder = new HttpClientBuilder(); 
httpClientBuilder
    .socketTimeout(2, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
    .establishConnectionTimeout(1, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
```
will result inside [HttpClientBuilder ](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blame/6a020d93f15c5c03e9b76a5e02b51e5391aade6a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/httpclient/HttpClientBuilder.java#L252) in
```
RequestConfig requestConfig = RequestConfig.custom()
    .setConnectTimeout(1000)
    .setSocketTimeout(2000);
```

### Actual behavior

The time units are ignored and the amount is always in milliseconds.
For example:
```
HttpClientBuilder httpClientBuilder = new HttpClientBuilder(); 
httpClientBuilder
    .socketTimeout(2, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
    .establishConnectionTimeout(1, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
```
will result inside [HttpClientBuilder](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blame/6a020d93f15c5c03e9b76a5e02b51e5391aade6a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/httpclient/HttpClientBuilder.java#L252) in
```
RequestConfig requestConfig = RequestConfig.custom()
    .setConnectTimeout(1)
    .setSocketTimeout(2);
```
This will fail very fast!

### How to Reproduce?

```
import org.apache.http.impl.client.CloseableHttpClient;
import org.keycloak.broker.provider.util.SimpleHttp;
import org.keycloak.connections.httpclient.HttpClientBuilder;

import java.io.IOException;
import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;

class Scratch {
    public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException {
        HttpClientBuilder httpClientBuilder = new HttpClientBuilder();
        httpClientBuilder
                .socketTimeout(2, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
                .establishConnectionTimeout(1, TimeUnit.SECONDS);
        CloseableHttpClient httpClient = httpClientBuilder.build();
        try (SimpleHttp.Response response = SimpleHttp.doGet("https://httpbin.org/get", httpClient).asResponse()) {
            System.out.println(response.asString());
        }
    }
}
```

Will fail almost immediately with:

```
Exception in thread "main" org.apache.http.conn.ConnectTimeoutException: Connect to httpbin.org:443 [httpbin.org/52.200.250.127, httpbin.org/34.200.110.94, httpbin.org/54.242.212.250, httpbin.org/34.204.34.199, httpbin.org/52.203.147.106, httpbin.org/44.209.252.174, httpbin.org/3.234.161.234, httpbin.org/18.210.66.151] failed: Connect timed out
	at org.apache.http.impl.conn.DefaultHttpClientConnectionOperator.connect(DefaultHttpClientConnectionOperator.java:151)
	at org.apache.http.impl.conn.PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager.connect(PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager.java:376)
	at org.apache.http.impl.execchain.MainClientExec.establishRoute(MainClientExec.java:393)
	at org.apache.http.impl.execchain.MainClientExec.execute(MainClientExec.java:236)
	at org.apache.http.impl.execchain.ProtocolExec.execute(ProtocolExec.java:186)
	at org.apache.http.impl.execchain.RetryExec.execute(RetryExec.java:89)
	at org.apache.http.impl.execchain.RedirectExec.execute(RedirectExec.java:110)
	at org.apache.http.impl.client.InternalHttpClient.doExecute(InternalHttpClient.java:185)
	at org.apache.http.impl.client.CloseableHttpClient.execute(CloseableHttpClient.java:83)
	at org.apache.http.impl.client.CloseableHttpClient.execute(CloseableHttpClient.java:108)
	at org.apache.http.impl.client.CloseableHttpClient.execute(CloseableHttpClient.java:56)
	at org.keycloak.broker.provider.util.SimpleHttp.makeRequest(SimpleHttp.java:299)
	at org.keycloak.broker.provider.util.SimpleHttp.asResponse(SimpleHttp.java:229)
	at Scratch.main(scratch_3.java:15)
Caused by: java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Connect timed out
	at java.base/sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl.timedFinishConnect(NioSocketImpl.java:551)
	at java.base/sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl.connect(NioSocketImpl.java:602)
	at java.base/java.net.SocksSocketImpl.connect(SocksSocketImpl.java:327)
	at java.base/java.net.Socket.connect(Socket.java:633)
	at org.apache.http.conn.ssl.SSLConnectionSocketFactory.connectSocket(SSLConnectionSocketFactory.java:368)
	at org.apache.http.impl.conn.DefaultHttpClientConnectionOperator.connect(DefaultHttpClientConnectionOperator.java:142)
	... 13 more
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

socketTimeoutUnits and establishConnectionTimeoutUnits use TimeUnit set in HttpClientBuilder

Closes #28881

issue_title: Master realm can be deleted
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

It's possible to delete the `master` realm via admin-ui/-api.
This was not the case in recent versions and resulted in an error message. Starting with KC 24.x, it's possible again! This is also possible with the nightly build.

### Version

24.0.x, nightly

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When trying to delete the `master` realm, the request should result in an error.

### Actual behavior

The request to delete the `master` realm succeeds and afterwards, it's no more possible to manage Keycloak through the admin-ui.

### How to Reproduce?

- create a new, fresh, clean KC environment
- login as master admin
- go to `master` realm -> realm settings
- select "delete realm" in the upper right action drop-down
- realm is being deleted

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

It is possible that NPE would be thrown when exporting user policy and the user is not found using userProvider API.

Reportedly the NPE occurs at this line:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/2b8c895f718560efa219f0a60d56eb8b7a1f1320/authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/user/UserPolicyProviderFactory.java#L122

and after investigating the code it seems that if `userProvider.getUserById(realm, id)` returns `null` (which is the case the user is not found) and then `.getUsername()` would thow the exception.

18

- [ ] The issue is a regression

no NPE

### Actual behavior

NPE

There is no reproducer.

issue_title: Auto-build shouldn't warn about unavailable runtime options
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

When running Keycloak in auto-build mode, the Picoli class will show a warning when conditional runtime options are used.   





### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No warnings in auto-build around unavailable runtime options, as the user can't really change what they are doing here, as in auto-build both runtime and non-runtime options are passed to the CLI.

Warnings should indicate something that the user should change (which they  can't in this case)

### Actual behavior

```
 (main) The following used run time options are UNAVAILABLE and will be ignored during build time:
        - cache-remote-tls-enabled: Available only when remote host is set.
```


### How to Reproduce?

Note that first #28950 needs to be merged, then the following will reproduce it. AFAIK this is the first time a runtime option is making something conditional.

```
bin/kc.sh start --cache-remote-host=localhost --cache-remote-tls-enabled=false
```

### Anything else?

This happens only when runtime options are the source of the conditional, as here with the `--cache-remote-host`.
commit_message:
fix: remove runtime related warnings from rebuild (#29110)

closes: #28948

Signed-off-by: Steve Hawkins <shawkins@redhat.com>
